title: payment_watcher: zero-value block.height resets scan to tip-lookback silently
description: payment_watcher.rs run_watcher_loop() line 126 checks if last_scanned_height == 0 to set the initial scan start. After processing a block, last_scanned_height = block.height (line 172). If lightwalletd returns a malformed CompactBlock with height == 0, last_scanned_height resets to 0, causing the next iteration to recalculate start from tip - INITIAL_LOOKBACK. This re-scans up to 100 blocks, re-activating any subscriptions found in those blocks a second time. activate_subscription_atomic uses CASE to preserve the max expiry, so double-activation is not catastrophic, but the re-scan wastes effort and logs spurious 'confirmed payment' lines. Fix: guard against block.height == 0 (or block.height < last_scanned_height) and log an error rather than accepting the height.
